title: corylopsis glandulifera var. hypoglauca
intro: Corylopsis glandulifera var. hypoglauca, also known as the Korean Yellow Poplar, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and aromatic bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Corylopsis glandulifera var. hypoglauca include its ability to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
